}

{
    "MUST be set to 1": "The DUP flag MUST be set to 1 by the Client or Server when the Client or Server attempts to re-deliver a PUBLISH packet",
    "MUST be set to 0": "The DUP flag MUST be set to 0 for all QoS 0 messages",
    "is not propagated": "The value of the DUP flag from an incoming PUBLISH packet is not propagated when the PUBLISH packet is sent to subscribers by the Server",
    "MUST be determined solely": "its value MUST be determined solely by whether the outgoing PUBLISH packet is a retransmission",
    "cannot assume": "The receiver of an MQTT Control Packet that contains the DUP flag set to 1 cannot assume that the receiver has seen an earlier copy of this packet"
}

The receiver stores the Packet Identifier and initiates onward delivery of the Application Message, then sends a PUBREC packet with the Packet Identifier and Reason Code

ownership of the Application Message is transferred to the receiver
the receiver needs to perform all checks for conditions which might result in a forwarding failure (for example, quota exceeded, authorization issues, etc.) before accepting ownership

MUST: MUST respond with a SUBACK packet
MUST have the same Packet Identifier
MUST replace that existing Subscription with a new Subscription
MUST be re-sent

MUST handle that packet as if it had received a sequence of multiple SUBSCRIBE packets
MUST contain a Reason Code for each Topic Filter/Subscription Option pair
MUST either show the maximum QoS that was granted for that Subscription or indicate that the subscription failed
MUST be the minimum of the QoS of the originally published message and the Maximum QoS granted by the Server
Server should never send a duplicate of that Message
MUST NOT: MUST NOT be lost

is the responsibility of the Responder to make sure the Responder has the authority to subscribe to the request topic and publish to the Response Topic

MUST: MUST assign an unused Packet Identifier when the sender has a new Application Message to publish
MUST send a PUBLISH packet containing this Packet Identifier with QoS 2 and DUP flag set to 0
MUST treat the PUBLISH packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBREC packet from the receiver
MUST send a PUBREL packet when the sender receives a PUBREC packet from the receiver with a Reason Code value less than 0x80
MUST contain the same Packet Identifier as the original PUBLISH packet
MUST treat the PUBREL packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBCOMP packet from the receiver
MUST NOT re-send the PUBLISH once the sender has sent the corresponding PUBREL packet
MUST NOT apply Message expiry if a PUBLISH packet has been sent
MUST respond with a PUBREC containing the Packet Identifier from the incoming PUBLISH packet, having accepted ownership of the Application Message
MUST treat any subsequent PUBLISH packet that contains that Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message
MUST acknowledge any subsequent PUBLISH packet with the same Packet Identifier by sending a PUBREC
MUST NOT cause duplicate messages to be delivered to any onward recipients in this case
MUST respond to a PUBREL packet by sending a PUBCOMP packet containing the same Packet Identifier as the PUBREL
MUST treat any subsequent PUBLISH packet that contains that Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message
MUST continue the QoS 2 acknowledgement sequence even if the receiver has applied message expiry
MUST NOT: MUST NOT re-send the PUBLISH once the sender has sent the corresponding PUBREL packet
MUST NOT apply Message expiry if a PUBLISH packet has been sent
MUST NOT cause duplicate messages to be delivered to any onward recipients in this case

6 Using WebSocket as a network transport.data:
{
    "MQTT Control Packets MUST be sent in WebSocket binary data frames": "Mandatory",
    "the recipient MUST close the Network Connection": "Mandatory",
    "The receiver MUST NOT assume that MQTT Control Packets are aligned on WebSocket frame boundaries": "Prohibited",
    "The Client MUST include mqtt in the list of WebSocket Sub Protocols the Client offers": "Mandatory",
    "The WebSocket Subprotocol name selected and returned by the Server MUST be mqtt": "Mandatory"
}

MUST: The receiver of a PUBLISH Packet MUST respond with the packet as determined by the QoS in the PUBLISH Packet [MQTT-3.3.4-1]
The Server MUST deliver the message to the Client respecting the maximum QoS of all the matching subscriptions [MQTT-3.3.4-2]

If the Client specified a Subscription Identifier for any of the overlapping subscriptions the Server MUST send those Subscription Identifiers in the message which is published as the result of the subscriptions [MQTT-3.3.4-3]

If the Server sends a single copy of the message it MUST include in the PUBLISH packet the Subscription Identifiers for all matching subscriptions which have Subscription Identifiers, their order is not significant [MQTT-3.3.4-4]
If the Server sends multiple PUBLISH packets it MUST send, in each of them, the Subscription Identifier of the matching subscription if the subscription has a Subscription Identifier [MQTT-3.3.4-5]

The Client MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which the Client has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Server [MQTT-3.3.4-7]
The Client MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them [MQTT-3.3.4-8]

The Server MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which the Server has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Client [MQTT-3.3.4-9]
The Server MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them [MQTT-3.3.4-10]
MUST NOT: A PUBLISH packet sent from a Client to a Server MUST NOT contain a Subscription Identifier [MQTT-3.3.4-6]

The Client MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which the Client has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Server [MQTT-3.3.4-7]
The Client MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them [MQTT-3.3.4-8]

The Server MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which the Server has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Client [MQTT-3.3.4-9]
The Server MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them [MQTT-3.3.4-10]
PROHIBITED: It is a Protocol Error for a PUBLISH packet to contain any Subscription Identifier other than those received in SUBSCRIBE packet which caused the PUBLISH packet to flow
ERROR: A Topic Alias value of 0 or greater than the Maximum Topic Alias is a Protocol Error
If the packet has a zero length Topic Name field it is a Protocol Error
It is a Protocol Error for a PUBLISH packet to contain any Subscription Identifier other than those received in SUBSCRIBE packet which caused the PUBLISH packet to flow

MUST: MUST close the Network Connection
MUST include an Authentication Method Property with the same value as in the CONNECT packet
MUST NOT send an AUTH packet
MUST NOT send an Authentication Method in the CONNACK packet
MUST NOT send an AUTH packet to the Server
MUST NOT: MUST NOT send an AUTH packet
MUST NOT send an Authentication Method in the CONNACK packet
MUST NOT send an AUTH packet to the Server
SHOULD: SHOULD include an Authentication Data property in the CONNECT packet
SHOULD authenticate using some or all of the information in the CONNECT packet, TLS session, and Network Connection
